mission.title: open-webui: Feat: delete parts of conversation.
mission.description: Feat: delete parts of conversation.. Requested by 13+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
I often face a situation when a discussion diverges because a prompt mistake or necessity of some side questions. This adds unneeded parts to the context that I would rather remove.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Feat: delete parts of conversation.
steps.description:
This pull request introduces functionality allowing users to delete their messages from the chatbot conversation. This feature aims to improve the user experience by enabling the management of conversation flow, particularly useful for removing side questions, clarifications, or typos.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Feat: delete parts of conversation." is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: This pull request introduces functionality allowing users to delete their messages from the chatbot conversation. This feature aims to improve the user experience by enabling the management of conversation flow, particularly useful for removing side questions, clarifications, or typos.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with open-webui installed or the issue environment reproducible.
